steps.title: Determine plural vs. possessive
steps.content: The sentence describes placing more than one hand between the two antennas. Both "hands" and "antennas" are plain plurals — neither is showing possession, so neither needs an apostrophe.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
Choice B uses simple plurals: "hands" and "antennas." A uses possessive forms incorrectly, and C and D add stray apostrophes where no possession exists.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
